summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with a warm welcome and focused the message on the foundational promise of Romans 8:28: 'And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.' He titled the sermon 'God Will Work It Out,' encouraging the congregation that even in difficult circumstances, God is actively orchestrating events for their ultimate good and His glory. Pastor Travis illustrated this truth through biblical examples, including David facing Goliath, Joseph's journey from the pit to the palace, and Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in the fiery furnace, showing that what seems like an attack is often part of God's promotion and anointing process. He emphasized that God's ways of working things out are often different and higher than our own, requiring trust even when we don't understand. The message centered on three key responses: trusting God in His ways, thanking God in all circumstances, and turning to God in repentance. Pastor Travis used the story of Jonah as a powerful case study of running from God, the resulting storms, and God's faithful provision and second chances. He concluded with a call to surrender, inviting those who felt they had been running from God to stop and run to Him, reaffirming the core belief that God is always at work, always on time, and will work everything out for good.
mainPoints: Trust God in His ways, even when you don't understand, because His perspective and methods are higher than ours.
Thank God in all circumstances, recognizing that He is actively working for your good even in difficult situations.
Turn to God and repent, stopping the cycle of running from His will and embracing His second chances.

versesMentioned.text: And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.
Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.
Give thanks in all circumstances; for this is God’s will for you in Christ Jesus.

“For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways,” declares the Lord. “As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts.”

But God chose the foolish things of the world to shame the wise; God chose the weak things of the world to shame the strong. God chose the lowly things of this world and the despised things—and the things that are not—to nullify the things that are, so that no one may boast before him.

The word of the Lord came to Jonah son of Amittai: “Go to the great city of Nineveh and preach against it, because its wickedness has come up before me.” But Jonah ran away from the Lord and headed for Tarshish. He went down to Joppa, where he found a ship bound for that port. After paying the fare, he went aboard and sailed for Tarshish to flee from the Lord.
There is a way that appears to be right, but in the end it leads to death.
Now the Lord provided a huge fish to swallow Jonah, and Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
Repent, then, and turn to God, so that your sins may be wiped out, that times of refreshing may come from the Lord.

Then the word of the Lord came to Jonah a second time: “Go to the great city of Nineveh and proclaim to it the message I give you.” Jonah obeyed the word of the Lord and went to Nineveh. Now Nineveh was a very large city; it took three days to go through it.

Monday.scripture: Romans 8:28 (NIV) - 'And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him...'
Monday.action: Identify one current challenge and consciously choose to believe God is working it for your good.
Monday.prayer: Lord, I don't understand this situation, but I choose to trust that You are in control and working it out for good. Help my unbelief.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 3:5-6 (NIV) - 'Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding...'
Tuesday.action: When faced with a decision today, pause and ask God for wisdom instead of relying solely on your own logic.
Tuesday.prayer: God, Your ways are higher than mine. I surrender my need to understand everything. I trust You with the path ahead.

Wednesday.action: Write down three things you are thankful for, including one difficult circumstance.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, thank You for Your constant presence. I thank You even for the hard things, believing You are at work in them.

Thursday.scripture: Jonah 1:1-3 (NIV) - 'But Jonah ran away from the Lord...'
Thursday.action: Honestly reflect: Is there an area where you are running from God's will or a clear command from His Word?
Thursday.prayer: Search me, O God. Show me if I am running from You in any way. Give me the courage to stop and turn back.

Friday.scripture: Acts 3:19 (NIV) - 'Repent, then, and turn to God...'
Friday.action: Take a specific step of repentance. Confess a known sin to God and make a practical plan, with His help, to turn away from it.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I confess my sin of __________. I am sorry. I turn away from it and turn my heart fully back to You. Wipe my slate clean.

Saturday.scripture: Jonah 3:1-3 (NIV) - 'Then the word of the Lord came to Jonah a second time... Jonah obeyed...'
Saturday.action: Reflect on God's faithfulness in giving you second chances. Choose to obey Him in one thing you have previously hesitated to do.
Saturday.prayer: Thank You, God, for Your endless grace and second chances. This time, I choose to obey. Strengthen me to follow through on what You have asked.
